title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: Runs right out of the box. However, it stutters a lot and often completely locks up, requiring me to force quit the game or even reboot the computer. I'm lucky to get in 5-10 minutes of gameplay before a lockup happens. I tried disabling Esync but that didn't help.
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: Runs okay with 3.7-4, but is better with 3.16-4. Stutters somewhat frequently, better with newer versions of Proton. Audio is fine. Controller is fine. Very occasional lighting/texture issues, however it is hard to say if this is due to Proton or the inherent bugginess of the game (game has numerous bugs and issues under Windows).
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes: Runs very good. Did not notice any performance decrease compared to Windows version. Zero issues, butter smooth gameplay!
title: Homefront®: The Revolution

notes:
This is an update to my report from a few days back.

* The game has lower performance than expected. As I haven't played the Windows version, I'm not sure how much of that is to do with the game being poorly optimised and how much is to do with Proton. I can keep my framerate at or close to 60fps with high settings @ 1080p for the most part, but it will drop down to 40 odd fps here and there (not including the stuttering issue).

* The game stutters quite heavily a fair bit in some outdoor areas. Seems to be worse around areas with a lot of lighting effects. Disabling Esync can actually make the issue a little worse in my experience, so I leave it enabled.

* Some warm coloured lights emit a weird shimmering effect. It's uncommon to see and is a very minor issue. Not sure if this issue also exists on Windows, but just mentioning it for the sake of accuracy.

* Regarding the total lock ups/crashes in my previous report which happened after 5-10 minutes of gameplay and required force quitting the game or sometimes even rebooting the computer - I found that disabling the Steam fps counter helped massively with this issue. I've sometimes been able to play for hours at a time now without the issue happening. It still exists though, but is far less common now.

* Sometimes, mid game, my screen will just go blank. Sometimes this'll only last a few seconds then the image will come back and I can continue play. Sometimes the image will not come back and my monitor will report that there's no signal. Half the time I can Alt+F4 to force quit the game, but sometimes I can't do this and then have to try and bring up the Terminal with a keyboard shortcut (Alt+F1 or Alt+F2 on Mint) to fix the issue with some commands. Sometimes though, the screen will not come on because it continues to receive no signal, and sometimes I will just see graphical corruption, both cases requiring a hard reboot.

When I can access the Terminal, sometimes just a reboot command will fix the issue. But it's common I'll see a black screen after logging in when I should be seeing the desktop. In this situation, I've found that "sudo apt-get install gnome-shell" will often fix the issue (if gnome shell is already installed then I first have to uninstall it before installing it again "sudo apt-get remove gnome-shell"). If this doesn't fix the black screen, I then have to restore Mint from a Timeshift backup via a live usb version of Mint, then sometimes also reinstall the gnome shell again afterwards.

So it obviously seems that this game can screw up your system's installation/config somehow.

* Conclusion: The game is somewhat playable, sometimes quite nicely for an hour or so without these issues causing too much trouble. But sooner or later, it's gonna crash on you and potentially cause you to have to fix your computer. Most people will probably find it to be quite a frustrating experience.
